chunk_summary: India-specific pediatric metabolic risk data highlights prevalence of obesity (4–6%), dysglycemia (8–12%), lipid abnormalities, and MASLD burden, emphasizing the need for targeted clinical interventions and family education.
learning_objectives: Understand the prevalence of pediatric metabolic risk factors in India, including obesity, dysglycemia, and lipid abnormalities.
Analyze the role of national survey data in estimating metabolic risk among Indian children and adolescents.
Recognize the clinical significance of MASLD prevalence in obese pediatric populations and its impact on family adherence to lifestyle changes.
key_facts: Overweight/obesity prevalence in Indian adolescents (10–19 years) is estimated at 4–6%.
Prediabetes/diabetes prevalence in early adolescence is reported at 8–12%, varying by sex and thresholds.
Hypertriglyceridemia affects nearly one-third of younger school-age children (5–9 years).
Low HDL-C prevalence increases into late adolescence, often indicating early insulin resistance.
MASLD prevalence among obese Indian children is approximately one-third, based on systematic reviews.
reference_quote: Cardiometabolic risk factors among Indian adolescents from national survey data — JAMA Network Open: 'National survey analysis in adolescents (10–19 years) has estimated overweight/obesity around 4–6%, highlighting that even a “minority” prevalence translates to a large absolute burden given India’s population size.'
document_key_topics: Pediatric obesity and dysglycemia prevalence in India
Lipid abnormalities and insulin resistance in Indian children
MASLD burden among obese pediatric populations
